bullets.text: Q2 combined ratio was 93.3, with year-to-date at 92.9; net income up 174% for the quarter and 68% year-to-date, though comprehensive income comparisons were more modest at +154% and +9%.
Written premium grew 6% for Q2 and 7% year-to-date, driven heavily by higher average premium per policy from 2012 rate actions rather than new customer volume.
Direct auto new applications were up low single digits while Agency new applications lagged prior year by ~7%, consistent with expected outcomes following significant 2012 rate increases.
Year-to-date catastrophe losses totaled $130 million from fires, tornadoes, flooding, hail, and late snow; all events were manageable within product pricing, though severe for affected customers.
Modest downward rate adjustments were taken in several large states, including Florida where loss experience was favorable; ongoing PIP uncertainty in Florida complicated further pricing actions.
Portfolio year-to-date total return was 2.2% after rising interest rates reduced gains in June; short-duration positioning provided insulation as the yield curve steepened significantly during Q2.
Progressive was recognized as best insurance website for the 20th time by Keynote; mobile servicing capabilities were being advanced to match the company's quoting and acquisition leadership.
Snapshot policies and associated premium grew to 1.2 million and $1.8 billion respectively; two new Service Centers opened in New Jersey and California, with nine total planned for 2013.
Renewal offer acceptance declined as expected post-rate increases, with slow recovery underway; management focused on rebuilding retention in both Agency and Direct channels for H2 growth.
